title: Audit existing examples schema and behavior for reuse as curated source pool
description:
## Summary
Inspect `ScorecardExampleItem`, current dashboard flows, and ownership semantics to define how existing examples can serve as the curated source pool.

## Deliverables
- Document the current scorecard-vs-score ownership behavior.
- Resolve the ambiguity in how examples are currently used.
- Produce the exact contract for examples as a curated source pool for deterministic reference datasets.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting audit: mapping ScorecardExampleItem schema, ownership semantics, and dashboard usage; target output is an explicit contract for examples-as-curated-source-pool and resolution of scorecard-vs-score ambiguity.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
## Audit Result: ScorecardExampleItem contract and usage

### Source-of-truth model contract
- Curated pool storage is `ScorecardExampleItem` in GraphQL schema (`dashboard/amplify/data/resource.ts`).
- Record shape: `scorecardId`, `itemId`, `addedAt`, `addedBy`, `notes`, plus `belongsTo(scorecard)` and `belongsTo(item)`.
- Scope is **scorecard-owned**, not score-owned. There is no `scoreId` on `ScorecardExampleItem`.
- Access patterns implemented: by `scorecardId`, by `itemId`, by `scorecardId+addedAt`.

### Runtime/UI contract in current code
- Dashboard and scorecard detail flows create/delete associations via `amplifyClient.ScorecardExampleItem` wrapper (`dashboard/utils/amplify-client.ts`).
- UI-facing `scorecard.examples` is derived/presented as string refs (`item:<itemId>`), not canonical storage.
- Canonical write path for curated item membership is create/delete in `ScorecardExampleItem`; UI array is a display/edit mirror.

### Ownership semantics
- Owner = scorecard context (`scorecardId`) under account scoping inherited from linked scorecard/item records.
- Item can be reused across scorecards by multiple association rows.
- `addedBy`/`notes` fields exist in schema but are not currently populated by active dashboard flows.

### Scorecard-vs-score ambiguity resolution
- Ambiguity observed: component naming uses `score` variable for scorecard objects in `ScorecardComponent`.
- Actual behavior is unambiguous at data layer: examples are scorecard-level curated pool entries.
- Therefore the explicit contract is: **examples are maintained at scorecard level, then consumed downstream for score-specific dataset builds by filtering/selection logic in later pipeline stages.**

### Operational notes for follow-on implementation
- Because table uniqueness is not enforced on (`scorecardId`,`itemId`) at schema level, duplicate prevention is currently app-side check; keep this risk visible for follow-on hardening.
- If association deletion fails, UI may still remove the local list entry; canonical source remains `ScorecardExampleItem`.

This satisfies the task deliverable: explicit contract for examples as curated source pool and resolution of scorecard-vs-score semantics.
